candidates.intentSummary: After any OpenClaw config patch or hand-edit, verify all agent peer bindings are still intact and surface any that are missing.
triggerPhrases: warum wird die binding config immer überschrieben WTF
proposedSteps: Read bindings[] from the live OpenClaw config via gateway config.get.
List all active bindings with agentId, peer.kind, and peer.id.
Compare against expected bindings from MEMORY.md / infra notes.
If any expected binding is absent: surface the specific missing entry and recovery config.patch command.

observations.intentSummary: When the GitHub Copilot OAuth token refreshes, copy the new token into all three agent auth-profile files (main, gh-sonnet, gh-haiku) to prevent agents from silently falling back to the wrong model.
triggerPhrases: gh-sonnet antwortete mit MiMo statt Claude Sonnet 4.6
Bei nächstem OAuth Token-Refresh muss das neue Token in alle 3 Auth-Files
proposedSteps: Check current token in main/agent/auth-profiles.json for github-copilot provider.
Copy token into gh-sonnet/agent/auth-profiles.json and gh-haiku/agent/auth-profiles.json.
Confirm all three agents respond with the correct model (claude-sonnet-4.6 / claude-haiku-4-5).
observations.reason: Only 1 distinct day — sub-threshold. Watch: if the next OAuth refresh repeats the same manual sync, this will qualify on the next scan.

observations.intentSummary: Review and decide on incoming external PRs to the OpenCami repository, including applying minor fixes before merging or rejecting oversized PRs.
observations.reason: Only 2 distinct days; no new activity in the extended window; coverage overlap with pr-pipeline and github-issue-fixer. Pattern fading — may drop from observations next scan if no recurrence.

notes: Ledger for skillminer. Every nightly scan mutates `candidates[]`, replaces `observations[]`, sets `last_scan`. `rejected[]` and `deferred[]` block re-proposal for 30 days via semantic (not just slug) match — entries REQUIRE `intentSummary` and `triggerPhrases`. `silenced[]` blocks re-proposal permanently (no expiry). `observations[]` is scan-scoped — fully replaced each scan with the current scan's sub-threshold patterns; a human can run `manage-ledger.sh promote <id>` to pull one into `candidates[]` as pending. Preferred mutation path: `scripts/manage-ledger.sh accept|reject|defer|promote|silence|unsilence <id> [reason]` — deterministic, atomic, copies required fields. Hand-editing state.json also works: set `status` on the candidate to accepted/rejected/deferred and the scan prompt (Step 3) + morning-write sweep both respect the new state.

candidate.intentSummary: one-sentence description of the underlying task (used for semantic cooldown matching)
candidate.occurrences: integer — COUNT IN THE MOST RECENT SCAN WINDOW (not cumulative lifetime)
candidate.confidence: high|medium|low — set at initial proposal, never mutated by later scans
candidate.coverageRisk: true when a capability-overlapping skill exists but no trigger-level duplicate

observation.occurrences: integer — count in current scan window
observation.previousOccurrences: null | integer — prior scan's observation count for the same semantic pattern; null means new or legacy data without trend history
observation.previousDays: null | integer — prior scan's distinct-day count for the same semantic pattern; null means new or legacy data without trend history
